title: render: layout inheritance recompiles inner+layout per render
body:
src/render/engine.ts:renderRoute recompiles both inner and layout Handlebars templates on every route. With N routes × M layouts that's wasted CPU at build time and a hot path during dev rebuilds.

**Fix**: cache compiled templates and layout pairs on engine init; key by template name.

title: render: template-as-partial registration collides with same-named partial
body: engine.ts:registerPartials registers every template (index, post, page, ...) under its bare name as a partial. A theme with a partial named `index` (legit Ghost theme pattern) would silently overwrite or be overwritten by the template registration. Namespace partials under a separate prefix and resolve `{{> name}}` accordingly.

title: render: ctx.post is also set for page routes, breaking {{#post}}/{{#page}} disambiguation
body:
src/render/engine.ts:buildContext sets `ctx.post = data.page` when rendering a page. Ghost themes use `{{#post}}` and `{{#page}}` as mutually exclusive context blocks. Setting both means a theme that branches on `{{#post}}` would incorrectly enter that branch on a static page.

**Fix**: set only the relevant context, not both.

title: render: post-as-partial does not exist for {{> post}} (custom layouts)
body: Some Ghost themes use `{{> post}}` from a custom layout to render the post body. We register templates as partials, so this works in some cases but uses the raw template source not the body-after-layout-split, leading to double layout rendering or compile errors.

title: content: slug derivation strips leading underscore filenames
body: src/content/loader.ts:slugFromPath uses `basename(filePath)`, then runs `slugify` which removes underscores. A file like `_index.md` collapses to empty string slug, then the post becomes inaccessible. Reject empty slugs at load time with a clear error pointing to the file.

title: content: empty title falls back to slug as title
body: loader.ts:normalizePost — if `data.title` is missing, we use slug as title. That produces titles like `hello-nectar` instead of erroring. Either error out (preferred) or title-case it. Currently it's silently weird.

title: content: asDateISO falls back to epoch 1970 with no warning
body: src/content/frontmatter.ts:asDateISO returns `new Date(0).toISOString()` when a date string is unparseable. Posts with bad dates silently sort to the bottom of the feed instead of erroring. Surface a parse error mentioning the post path.

title: content: word_count counts whitespace-tokenised words, underestimates CJK by 90%+
body: markdown.ts:countWords splits on `/\s+/` — Japanese, Chinese, Thai etc. produce a count near 0 because they don't use ASCII whitespace between characters. Reading time for a 4000-character Japanese post would be ~1 minute. Use `Intl.Segmenter` with grapheme/word granularity for accurate CJK counts.

title: content: drafts have no preview path; no way for authors to preview unpublished work
body: We skip `status: draft` at build. To preview, an author would have to flip status and rebuild. Add a `nectar build --include-drafts` flag emitting to `dist/drafts/<slug>/index.html` with `noindex` and a banner.

title: content: scheduled posts not supported
body: Ghost has `status: scheduled` posts that publish at a future timestamp. We accept the status value but treat it like published. Either filter them out at build (default) or build with a `publishAt` time check + a `--rebuild-at <ISO>` hint.

title: content: tag/author auto-creation does not warn
body: loader.ts resolves unknown tag/author slugs into stub objects silently. A typo in `tags: [neews]` creates a phantom `neews` tag with a single post and no description. Emit a warning when this happens (per slug, deduped).

title: render: {{img_url size=}} returns the same URL for every srcset entry
body: helpers/assets.ts:imgUrlHelper recognises `size=` but doesn't actually pick a resized variant. The result is a `srcset` that lists the same source URL six times — wastes bandwidth on each fetch and disables responsive selection. Implement actual image resizing (separate issue) and emit unique URLs per size.

title: render: {{img_url absolute=true}} broken for external URLs
body: imgUrlHelper passes `candidate` to `new URL(url, siteUrl)` only when relative. But the early `if (size && ...)` path always normalises to a leading slash, breaking absolute external URLs. Detect external before normalising.

title: render: {{asset 'path'}} double-encodes URL when path contains %
body: helpers/assets.ts:assetHelper joins the base path then runs `escapeHtml` over the result. Filenames containing `&`, `'`, `"`, `<`, `>` are escaped, but a URL with `%20` is fine — however a path with literal `%` would get re-encoded by escapeHtml's `&` rule indirectly via `&`. Use proper URL handling (`encodeURI` once at construction) rather than HTML-escaping.

title: render: foreach `visibility` filter does not apply when filtering posts
body: helpers/blocks.ts:foreachHelper applies visibilityFilter only on items with a `visibility` field. Tags and authors have no visibility; the filter passes them through. Document this and add explicit support for `visibility="public"` filtering tags by `tag.visibility==='public'`.

title: render: foreach `from=`/`to=` evaluate on visible-then-sliced data (Ghost slices first)
body: Our foreach does `visibilityFilter(items).slice(from-1, to)`. Ghost's behaviour: it slices the input first, then filters. Worth verifying against Ghost's reference impl; can produce off-by-one when public+members posts are interleaved.

title: render: foreach data frame missing `rowStart`/`rowEnd` for grid layouts
body: Several Ghost themes use `@rowStart` / `@rowEnd` for masonry/grid wrapping. We set them to `false` unconditionally. Compute them from a `columns=` hash option (default 1) on the foreach call.

title: render: foreach with empty input falls through inverse but emits no `{{else}}` block separator
body: Sanity check: when items array is empty, we call `options.inverse(this)`. Verify this is exposed via `{{else}}` in foreach (Ghost docs say it is). Add a test.

title: render: `is` helper does not recognise multi-target with space (Ghost accepts both ',' and space)
body: Ghost themes write `{{#is "post page"}}` and `{{#is "post, page"}}`. We split on `,` only. Split on `[,\s]+`.

title: render: `is` helper's `paged` check uses route.data.pagination but list views without pagination still set page=1; correct
body: Verify that pages without pagination (single post route) don't return true for `{{#is "paged"}}`. Currently `route.data.pagination` is undefined for posts → ok, but for indexes with 1 page also ok. Add explicit test.

title: render: `has` helper supports only first matched hash key; multi-key should AND not bail
body: helpers/blocks.ts:hasHelper loops keys and `break`s on first match. Ghost's behaviour for `{{#has tag="x" author="y"}}` is an OR of provided constraints (default). With `any:` / `all:` prefixes it changes. Implement Ghost's semantics: parse `key:value` pairs from a single `match=` argument and honour `count:>N` and `slug:[a,b]`.

title: render: `has` helper does not support count comparators (count:>0)
body: Real Ghost themes write `{{#has count:"tags">0}}`. Not implemented.

title: render: `match` helper does not coerce string "true"/"false" booleans
body: `{{#match @custom.background_image "true"}}` — @custom comes through as boolean from package.json, but theme writes the string. Implement implicit string<->bool comparison.

title: render: `match` 3-arg numeric operators force Number(left) > Number(right) on strings
body: Comparing strings like `{{#match posts.length ">=" 4}}` works because posts.length is a number. But `{{#match foo ">" bar}}` with both strings gets `NaN > NaN === false`. Document this and add a `compareAs` hash option, or detect string vs number.

title: render: `get` helper ignores `include=` parameter (authors, tags, count)
body: Our Post model already has full authors/tags hydrated, so `include="authors"` is a no-op. That's fine for now, but `include="count.posts"` (Ghost's tag/author counts) is silently dropped. Resolve counts on the way out.

title: render: `get` helper filter DSL is a tiny subset of NQL
body: Ghost uses NQL (https://ghost.org/docs/content-api/#filtering). We support `key:value`, `key:-value`, `key:[a,b]` and `+` AND. Missing: `,` OR, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`, `~`, `~^`, `~$`, quoted strings, escapes, parens, `null`. Implement NQL grammar properly.

title: render: `get` filter interpolation only expands {{post.id}} when ctx is post; misses nested
body: blocks.ts:evaluateClause interpolates `{{post.id}}` from `this` (the surrounding context). On a non-post page, post is undefined and the interpolated value is empty, returning all posts. Either pass the route context or error.

title: render: `get` does not return Pagination metadata to the block
body: Ghost's `{{#get}}` exposes a `pagination` object to the block. We expose only the array. Themes that paginate inside `{{#get}}` cannot.

title: render: `get` mutates input arrays via slice().sort() — fine, but does not deep-clone
body: If a downstream helper mutates a Post inside the block, it mutates our content graph. Freeze content models with `Object.freeze` after load, or deep-clone in `get`.

title: render: `date` helper does not honour Ghost's `timeago` semantics (number unit)
body: Our timeago path calls `dayjs(value).fromNow()`. Verify it matches Ghost's output (e.g., "a day ago" vs "1 day ago"). Probably fine; needs a test.

title: render: `date` helper inflates timezone before tz plugin loaded if module ordering shifts
body: We `dayjs.extend(timezone)` at module top; rely on import side-effects. If tree-shaking removes the side-effect, `dayjs(value).tz(...)` throws. Add a runtime guard / test that `dayjs.tz` is defined.

title: render: `t` helper falls back to key when missing; but if active locale exists with empty string, returns empty
body: helpers/i18n.ts: lookup chain is active[key] ?? fallback[key] ?? key. An empty string is NOT nullish, so an intentionally empty translation (like Source theme's `"Featured": ""`) gets returned. That's actually correct Ghost behavior — but it surprises users. Document.

title: render: `t` helper interpolates {key} once but doesn't escape; injecting HTML risk via i18n
body: Translation strings often contain `%`. We pass them through `new SafeString`? No — we return a raw string. If a theme uses `{{t}}` inline without triple-braces, the HTML is escaped. With triple, XSS surface from locale files. Document.

title: render: `authors` inline rendering does not honour `separator=`/`autolink=`/`limit=`/`from=` like Ghost
body: helpers/content.ts:authorsHelper inline returns `list.map(a=>a.name).join(', ')`. Ghost supports `separator`, `lastSeparator`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `autolink`, `limit`, `from`, `to`. Implement these.

title: render: `tags` inline renders <a> in HTML escape mode; `autolink=false` works but no `prefix`/`suffix`/`limit`
body: Same gap as `authors`. Implement Ghost's full `tags` helper signature.

title: render: `reading_time` uses 275 wpm regardless of locale
body: Ghost's algorithm: 275 wpm + 12 sec per image. We don't count images. With CJK we should switch units (chars / chars-per-min). Implement a locale-aware reading time.

title: render: `meta_title` does not handle Ghost's title delimiters per route
body: Ghost themes commonly do `{{meta_title}} | {{@site.title}}` themselves. Our implementation falls back to title alone. That's fine, but `page=" (Page %)"` suffix only applies on listing routes. Verify across home/index/tag/author.

title: render: `meta_description` returns empty string when missing; Ghost returns first sentence of content
body: Improve fallback chain: meta_description → custom_excerpt → og_description → excerpt → site.description → first sentence of plaintext.

title: render: `ghost_head` does not emit pagination rel=prev/rel=next
body: On a paginated index, `<link rel="prev" href="...">` and `<link rel="next" href="...">` improve SEO. Emit them from ghost_head when route.data.pagination exists.

title: render: `ghost_head` does not emit RSS feed discovery link
body: `<link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" href="/rss.xml">` is missing. Browser RSS detection and feed-reader autodiscovery require it.

title: render: `ghost_head` JSON-LD `Article` schema missing required fields
body: Google validator requires `mainEntityOfPage`, `publisher.logo.width/height`, `image` as object with width/height. Our payload is missing those. Use https://search.google.com/test/rich-results to verify and fix.

title: render: `ghost_head` JSON-LD does not encode special characters before JSON.stringify
body: If a post title contains `</script>`, JSON.stringify won't escape `<`. Embed the JSON-LD with `</` escaped to `<\/` to prevent script-out injection.

title: render: `ghost_foot` outputs unescaped codeinjection_foot — intentional, but document risk
body: Raw HTML by design. Document this as the one explicit XSS exit (only authors can edit content). Recommend CSP nonces.

title: render: `navigation` helper renders bespoke HTML even if a `navigation.hbs` partial exists
body: Source theme provides `partials/components/navigation.hbs`. When the theme has a navigation partial, prefer rendering that partial; only fall back to our minimal output when missing.

title: render: `pagination` helper renders bespoke HTML; should defer to partials/pagination.hbs when present
body: Same as navigation — Source's theme provides one. Use it when available.

title: render: `pagination` helper does not include `page_url` blockParam style
body: Ghost exposes `{{#pagination}}{{this.page_url}}{{/pagination}}` block form. Implement.

title: render: `body_class` does not include `tag-hash-x` markers for internal tags
body: Ghost's body_class includes tag tokens. We add `tag-<slug>` only on tag routes. Add per-post tag tokens too, and special-case internal tags (prefixed with `#`).

title: render: `post_class` does not include `image` / `no-image`, `featured`, `no-content`, page-specific tokens
body: Ghost's post_class is richer. Add tokens: `image` when feature_image exists, `no-image` otherwise, `no-content` when content empty.

title: render: `concat` does not honour `separator` when given Handlebars SafeString
body: concatHelper calls `String()` on each value. SafeString.toString() returns the wrapped string — fine. Edge case: when SafeString contains unescaped HTML, concat strips that information (downstream re-escapes). Mark concat result as SafeString when all inputs were SafeString.

title: render: `social_url` mastodon assumes mastodon.social when host omitted
body: Ghost themes pass `@user@host.tld` — we split correctly. But when only `@user` is given, defaulting to `mastodon.social` is presumptuous. Either return empty (require fully-qualified) or document.

title: render: `social_url` does not support `discord`, `mastodon` profile URLs as-is
body: If author.mastodon is already a URL, our builder wraps it as `https://mastodon.social/@<URL>` — broken. Detect existing URLs.

title: render: missing helpers — `cancel_link`, `comment_count`, `member_count`, `signup`, `tiers`, `price`
body: Themes that use members features call these. We don't implement them. Either stub safely (return empty) so themes don't crash, or implement.

title: render: missing helper — `recommendations`
body: Source theme uses `{{recommendations}}` in the sidebar. Currently unimplemented — Handlebars emits a 'missing helper' warning or crash depending on version. Stub.

title: render: missing helper — `access`
body: Themes use `{{#unless access}}` to gate content for non-members. Without it, the lock icon path in post-card.hbs may crash.

title: render: missing helper — `next_post` / `prev_post`
body: Some themes use them (in addition to `post.prev`/`post.next`). Stub.

title: render: missing helper — `lookup`
body: Ghost's `{{lookup obj key}}` does dynamic property access. Useful for theme writers.

title: render: missing helper — `raw`
body: Outputs raw HTML, similar to triple-mustache. Used in some themes.

title: render: missing helper — `data`
body: Ghost has a `{{#data}}` block for loading additional content. We don't implement it.

title: render: missing helper — `image_dimensions`
body: Used by themes that emit width/height attributes. Requires reading image dimensions at build (`probe-image-size` or `sharp.metadata`).

title: render: missing helper — `is_active`
body: Some Casper-style themes use this for nav highlighting. Implement as alias for our link_class.

title: build: rm -rf outputDir destroys any user-managed files (e.g., a CNAME)
body: src/build/pipeline.ts deletes the output dir entirely. Users hosting on GitHub Pages keep CNAME and `_headers` etc. there. Either: (a) write a `.nectarignore`-aware preserve list, or (b) clean only files we wrote in the previous build (track via a manifest).

title: build: route output paths can collide silently (post slug == page slug)
body: A post 'about' and a page 'about' both emit `about/index.html`. Last one wins. Detect collisions and error with both file paths.

title: build: emit.ts copies both fingerprinted and logical asset paths (2x disk)
body: Production builds should only emit fingerprinted versions. Logical ones are useful for development and for compatibility with hand-written URLs in content; gate behind `[build].emit_logical_asset_aliases` flag (default false in production).

title: build: sequential rendering — N posts = N awaits in serial
body: pipeline.ts loops `for (route of routes) { engine.render(route); writeHtml(...) }`. Parallelise with `Promise.all` chunked at CPU count. For 1000-post sites this is the difference between 3s and 30s.

title: build: no incremental builds; full rebuild every time
body: Watch mode and large sites need content/template invalidation. Hash inputs per route; skip rerender when hash matches manifest.

title: build: asset hashing reads whole file into memory
body: theme/assets.ts reads each file via readFile(). For big images/videos that's RAM-heavy. Stream via Bun.file().stream() into the hasher.

title: build: asset fingerprint hash is sha1 truncated to 10 hex chars
body: sha1 is broken cryptographically but fine for cache-busting. The 10-char prefix has ~40 bits — birthday collision odds at 10k files = nontrivial. Switch to base64url of sha256 first 12 chars.

title: build: feeds.ts double-prefixes site URL when post.url is absolute
body: feeds.ts:emitRss does `base + new URL(post.url).pathname`. If post.url is already absolute, this is correct. But for the `<guid>`, Ghost uses a stable ID — ours regenerates per-build. Use post.id (e.g. `post-<slug>`).

title: build: RSS lacks atom:link self, channel image, language for content
body: Add `<atom:link href=".../rss.xml" rel="self" type="application/rss+xml"/>`, `<image>` if site.logo, `<lastBuildDate>`.

title: build: RSS includes full content:encoded; some prefer summary-only
body: Add `[components.rss].full_content = true|false` flag.

title: build: sitemap has no lastmod, changefreq, priority; single-file only
body: Per sitemap protocol, add lastmod (use updated_at). For >50k URLs Ghost splits into sitemap-posts/-pages/-tags/-authors with a sitemap index. Implement multi-file emission.

title: build: no robots.txt emitted
body: Default emit `User-agent: *\nAllow: /\nSitemap: <site>/sitemap.xml`. Allow override via `static/robots.txt`.

title: build: no 404.html emitted
body: Most hosts serve `404.html` for unknown paths. Emit one from a theme template (`error-404.hbs` or `error.hbs`), fall back to a built-in.

title: build: no static/ passthrough directory
body: Many SSGs let users drop arbitrary files into `static/` and pass them through unchanged. We don't. Add `[content].static_dir`.

title: build: pagination URLs hard-code '/page/N/' — Ghost allows custom pagination prefix
body: Routes.ts hard-codes `/page/N/`. Ghost lets themes override via routes.yaml. Make configurable.

title: build: tag/author archive routes always exist — Ghost themes can suppress them via routes.yaml
body: Implement basic routes.yaml support: disabling tag or author collections; custom paths (e.g. `/category/` instead of `/tag/`).

title: build: no redirects.yaml support
body: Ghost emits `_redirects` (Netlify) or nginx config from `redirects.yaml`. Implement.

title: build: home and index treat as the same template but URLs collide
body: If `home.hbs` exists we route `/` to it; pagination uses index. If only `index.hbs` exists we route `/` to it. Confirm: when both exist, page 2 should go through index, not home. Test.

title: config: posts_per_page in nectar.toml is overridden by theme pkg.json
body: routes.ts uses `theme.pkg.posts_per_page || config.build.posts_per_page`. User intent likely the other way — user config overrides theme defaults. Flip.

title: config: schema does not validate theme custom-setting types against theme package.json
body: User can set `nectar.toml [theme.custom].title_font = 'Comic Sans'` and we'll accept it even though Source's enum only allows 3 values. Validate against pkg.custom definitions.

title: config: site.url must end without trailing slash; we don't normalise
body: URL joins use `endsWith('/')` to decide. A user supplying `https://example.com//` breaks. Normalise on load.

title: config: navigation entries lack icon/external-link/target metadata
body: Real Ghost themes can read additional nav metadata. Extend schema.

title: cli: `new` always lowercases slugs via strict slugify — losing CJK info
body: `slugify('日本語')` returns empty. Use `slug` package with transliterate or accept the original as-is when ASCII percent-encode is acceptable.

title: cli: `new` does not check existing file; overwrites silently
body: Race against itself if user runs twice. Add `--force` flag and otherwise refuse to overwrite.

title: cli: `serve` doesn't watch; users have to restart after edits
body: Wire up `fs.watch` over content/ and themes/, debounce, rebuild, send reload signal via Server-Sent Events to a tiny client snippet.

title: cli: no `dev` subcommand combining build + watch + serve + reload
body: Standard expectation in modern SSGs. Add.

title: cli: error stack traces dumped to stderr without source maps
body: Production-builds map back to TypeScript via Bun's built-in support. Confirm `--source-map=external` or inline works; add to CI smoke.

title: cli: no progress reporter for builds
body: For sites with many posts, the user has no feedback. Add a simple spinner+counter (`Rendering 12/150...`).

title: cli: no `--json` output mode for machine integration
body: CI/CD pipelines want structured output. Add `--format=json` to all subcommands.

title: cli: `import-ghost` does not download images from Ghost CDN
body: Image URLs in imported posts point to the original Ghost host. Add `--download-images` to fetch and rewrite paths.

title: cli: `import-ghost` accepts only a single export file
body: Ghost's CLI export can split DBs into multiple files. Accept a directory.

title: cli: `import-ghost` does not preserve Mobiledoc / Lexical content
body: Only HTML import works. Add Lexical→HTML via `@tryghost/kg-lexical-html-renderer`. Mobiledoc via `@tryghost/kg-mobiledoc-html-renderer`.

title: cli: `serve` does not select an open port if 4321 is taken
body: Bun.serve throws. Try 4321, then 4322, ... up to 4400; log the chosen port.

title: cli: `check` does not actually compile templates
body: We load templates as strings only. Compile them to surface syntax errors with file/line context.

title: cli: `check` does not validate frontmatter against schema
body: Add a strict schema check (required title, valid dates, known status enum, known visibility enum). Emit a JSON report.

title: cli: no shell completions (zsh/fish/bash)
body: Use `commander`/`yargs` or hand-roll. Generate via `nectar completions <shell>`.

title: cli: no `version --json` and no version stability promise
body: Document SemVer policy and stability of public surface.

title: ghost-import: no slug sanitisation against path traversal
body: import.ts writes to `content/posts/<slug>.md`. If a malicious export contains `slug: "../../etc/passwd"`, we'd traverse. Validate slug matches `/^[a-z0-9-]+$/`.

title: ghost-import: turndown is not configured for Ghost-specific cards (bookmark, gallery, callout, signup)
body: Ghost cards have specific markup that turndown converts to gibberish or drops. Add per-card converters.

title: ghost-import: writes content with double newlines; not idempotent across runs
body: Body trailing newline + frontmatter trailing newline = inconsistent. Define a canonical output format.

title: ghost-import: does not import settings (site title, description, navigation) from Ghost
body: Ghost's export includes `settings: {...}`. Map to `nectar.toml` on import (with a confirmation).

title: ghost-import: tags imported only if they have description/feature_image/meta_title
body: Otherwise auto-inferred. Importer should optionally emit all tags so users can edit them.

title: ghost-import: no detection of duplicate slugs between posts/pages
body: If a Ghost site has a post and page with same slug, we overwrite. Detect and report.

title: ghost-import: HTML→Markdown lossy on code blocks with language hints
body: turndown drops the language. Configure with `codeBlockStyle: 'fenced'` AND a custom rule for `<pre><code class="language-xxx">`.

title: ghost-import: missing JSON Schema validation of the export file
body: Right now we trust the shape and access nested properties. Validate top-level structure and report clear errors for partial/corrupt exports.

title: ghost-import: no dry-run mode
body: Add `--dry-run` printing the plan (N posts, M pages, ...). Useful before nuking content/.

title: theme-loader: locale parse failures are silently swallowed
body: src/theme/loader.ts:loadLocales has `try { JSON.parse } catch {}` that ignores broken locale files. Print a warning.

title: theme-loader: only string values are kept; numbers/booleans in locales dropped silently
body: Some themes store interpolation counters. Either keep all values or document the constraint.

title: theme-loader: partials cannot include / via ../ — by design — but no clear error
body: Document.

title: theme-loader: theme custom-settings group/visibility are parsed but unused
body: Once a UI exists they matter. Today, no UI. Capture and expose to a future admin component.

title: theme: no `nectar theme:zip` to package a theme for distribution
body: Source theme has `gulp zip`. Add equivalent.

title: theme: no `nectar theme:new` scaffolder
body: Add a CLI that creates a minimal theme skeleton.

title: theme: no theme:lint analog of Ghost's gscan
body: Validate that referenced helpers exist, partials resolve, custom settings are well-formed.

title: test: no helper-level unit tests for any individual helper
body: Every helper in src/render/helpers/ needs at least one happy-path and one edge-case test.

title: test: no golden test for Source theme rendering
body: Add a golden-HTML test that builds the example and diffs against committed expected output. Catches regressions in any helper.

title: test: no fixture-based test for ghost-import
body: Add a minimal Ghost JSON fixture (5 posts, 2 pages, 3 tags, 2 users with cross-references) and assert importer outputs.

title: test: no CLI integration tests
body: Spawn `bun src/cli/index.ts <command>` and assert exit codes / stdout/stderr.

title: test: no coverage reporting
body: Bun supports `bun test --coverage`. Wire into CI and threshold at e.g. 70% lines.

title: test: no property-based / fuzz tests for Markdown/frontmatter
body: Use `fast-check` (works in Bun) to fuzz frontmatter parsing.

title: test: no Lighthouse / a11y CI gate
body: Run `@axe-core/cli` over example/dist/ and fail on serious issues.

title: infra: no .github/workflows for CI
body: Add a workflow running typecheck + bun test + biome check on PRs to main.

title: infra: no Renovate / Dependabot configuration
body: Add `renovate.json` or `.github/dependabot.yml`.

title: infra: no release automation
body: Use release-please / semantic-release. Tag → GitHub Release → npm publish + binary upload.

title: infra: package is `private: true` — cannot npm publish today
body: Flip when ready and add `files`, `bin`, `publishConfig`.

title: infra: no single-file binary build
body: Use `bun build --compile` to produce `nectar` binary per platform.

title: infra: no Docker image
body: Provide an Alpine-based image baking in bun + nectar.

title: infra: no Homebrew tap
body: Add a tap (`oven-sh/bun` style) for `brew install nectar`.

title: infra: no CHANGELOG.md
body: Generate from conventional commits via release-please.

title: docs: no CONTRIBUTING.md
body: Describe dev setup, branching, commit style, testing expectations.

title: docs: no CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
body: Adopt Contributor Covenant 2.1.

title: docs: no SECURITY.md
body: Define disclosure address and process; private vulnerability reporting via GitHub Security Advisories.

title: docs: no issue templates
body: Add `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug,feature,ghost-compat}.yml`.

title: docs: no PR template
body: Add `.github/pull_request_template.md` with summary / test plan / breaking change checkboxes.

title: docs: no architecture diagram in DESIGN.md beyond ASCII art
body: Add SVG diagrams via Mermaid.

title: docs: no config reference auto-generated from Zod schema
body: Use `zod-to-json-schema` + a script to emit `docs/config-reference.md` and keep it in sync via CI.

title: docs: no `nectar` CLI reference page
body: Auto-emit usage docs from each command's help output.

title: docs: no theme dev guide
body: Document our Ghost compatibility surface, helper signatures, partial conventions, asset pipeline. With examples.

title: docs: no migration guide (long form) for Ghost users
body: Step-by-step: export from Ghost, run import, copy images, edit nectar.toml, run build, deploy. Include screenshots.

title: docs: no published docs site
body: Use Nectar to build its own docs site (dogfood).

title: api: no public extension API
body: DESIGN mentions optional components but no actual API. Define hook points (beforeBuild, afterContentLoad, afterRender, afterEmit), helper registration, route augmentation, transform pipeline.

title: api: types not exported from a stable entrypoint
body: Add `nectar/types` exporting Post/Page/Tag/Author/SiteData/NectarConfig.

title: api: no plugin discovery convention (`nectar-plugin-*` packages)
body: Pick one — naming convention + import contract — and document.

title: observability: no JSON log mode for CI / log shippers
body: Add NECTAR_LOG_FORMAT=json mode in logger.ts.

title: observability: no per-route timing report
body: Build summary should include slowest routes, helper hotspots.

title: observability: no error reporting integration (Sentry/Bugsnag)
body: Provide hooks; document configuration.

title: i18n: only one locale per build; no multi-locale routing
body: Many international blogs need `/en/foo/` and `/ja/foo/`. Define a content layout convention (per-locale subdirs) and route accordingly.

title: i18n: date format string is en-rooted (`DD MMM YYYY`)
body: Use Intl.DateTimeFormat with options derived from locale.

title: i18n: pluralisation in `t` helper is `%`-based, not ICU MessageFormat
body: Move to ICU when many locales are supported.

title: search: no Pagefind integration
body: Wire as an optional component that runs after emit and writes `dist/pagefind/`.

title: search: no fallback Lunr integration
body: For users who can't ship Pagefind binary.

title: search: no `<search>` widget partial in built-in helpers
body: Expose a default search UI partial themes can include.

title: comments: no Giscus / Disqus / Utterances wiring
body: Implement `[components.comments] provider = "giscus|disqus|utterances"` and inject the right snippet under `{{comments}}`.

title: members: no stub for @member context
body: Source theme reads `@member` extensively. Currently undefined; many template branches misbehave. Provide `@member = undefined` consistently and document.

title: members: no docs explaining members are out of scope
body: Users migrating from a Ghost site with paying members must know what they lose.

title: image-pipeline: no resizing of feature_image to image_sizes
body: Use sharp (optional) to produce per-size variants. Cache by content hash.

title: image-pipeline: no WebP/AVIF output
body: Modern formats save 30-50% bytes. Emit when source is jpg/png.

title: image-pipeline: no LQIP/blurhash for above-the-fold images
body: Improves CLS and perceived perf.

title: image-pipeline: no EXIF strip / SVG sanitisation on import
body: Privacy + XSS. Strip EXIF; sanitise SVGs via svgo or DOMPurify.

title: image-pipeline: image_dimensions not embedded as width/height attributes
body: Improves CLS. Compute at build via probe-image-size.

title: good-first-issue: add a `nectar version --check` to look up latest release
body: Lightweight network call to GitHub Releases; cached for 24h.

title: good-first-issue: add `--quiet` / `--verbose` flags to all commands
body: Bind to logger threshold.

title: good-first-issue: emit a build manifest (dist/.nectar/manifest.json) listing every file we wrote
body: Powers safe re-emit (vs rm -rf).

title: infra: no spell check on docs/Markdown content
body: Add cspell GitHub Action over docs/ and example/content/.

title: infra: no link checker for docs
body: Use lychee in CI.

title: infra: no LICENSE in src/ headers; just root LICENSE file
body: Optional but nice for users copying snippets.

title: infra: bun.lock not pinned to integrity-checked SHA per dep
body: Confirm `bun install --frozen-lockfile` in CI.

title: config: zod schema does not warn on unknown keys
body: Use `.strict()` to reject typos in nectar.toml — currently `[sites]` (typo) is silently ignored.

title: config: relative paths in config are not resolved against the config file location
body: If a user passes `--config path/to/nectar.toml`, our content/theme dirs resolve from cwd not from the config dir. Document or change.

title: config: no support for environment-variable overrides
body: E.g. NECTAR_SITE_URL useful for staging vs prod builds.

title: config: theme.dir is relative to cwd; cannot reference an installed npm theme
body: Allow `theme = "nectar-theme-foo"` to resolve from node_modules.

title: config: no validation that [theme.custom] keys exist in theme package.json
body: Typos like `[theme.custom].titel_font` silently use defaults. Warn.

title: performance: theme assets re-read for fingerprinting on every build
body: Cache by mtime + size in `.nectar/asset-cache.json`.

title: performance: Markdown rendering is sequential
body: Parallelise content load via Promise.all over file globs.

title: performance: handlebars compile per partial registration; pre-compile once at engine init
body: Partial source is registered as raw string; Handlebars compiles lazily on first use. For sites with many partials, pre-compile to AOT.

title: render: no nonce support for inline `<script>` and `<style>` in ghost_head
body: For CSP. Accept `[build].csp_nonce` and emit `nonce="..."` on our inline tags.

title: render: no Subresource Integrity (SRI) for asset links
body: Emit `integrity="sha384-..."` on external scripts/stylesheets where applicable.

title: render: HTML output not minified
body: Add optional minifier (html-minifier-terser) post-render.

title: render: CSS not purged/inlined
body: Source theme ships ~50KB of unused CSS. Optional purge step (purgecss).

title: render: JS asset bundling/minification not done
body: Source theme JS already minified; users with custom JS would want bun build pipeline.

title: ghost-compat: routes.yaml not parsed at all
body: Ghost's `routes.yaml` defines custom collections, channels, and routes. Major feature; required for many real themes.

title: ghost-compat: dynamic routing taxonomies
body: Tag/author taxonomy paths configurable in routes.yaml; we hard-code /tag/ and /author/.

title: ghost-compat: featured posts highlight on home requires `featured` filter in {{#get}}
body: Verified working in our example, but only because our content has featured posts. Test the empty case.

title: ghost-compat: visibility 'members' / 'paid' posts treated as 'public' in build
body: We have no auth so we render everything. Either: (a) skip non-public at build (closer to Ghost's free-tier behaviour with paywall preview) or (b) gate behind a toggle.

title: ghost-compat: @config.posts_per_page used by theme but only theme.pkg drives our value
body: Source theme reads `@config.posts_per_page`. Expose it.

title: ghost-compat: @labs context undefined; themes that check it crash
body: Expose as empty object so `{{#if @labs.foo}}` is safe.

title: ghost-compat: @site.members_enabled / paid_members_enabled / recommendations_enabled need stable defaults
body: Default to false; document. Source's sidebar branches on these.

title: ghost-compat: @site.lang vs @site.locale
body: Ghost themes use both. We currently set both to the same value via SiteData; document.

title: ghost-compat: time helper missing (`{{time format="..."}}` deprecated alias for date)
body: Some older themes still use it. Alias.

title: ghost-compat: encode helper supports `:component` vs `:full` modes
body: We always use encodeURIComponent. Ghost has variants.

title: build: emitter does not write atomically
body: Concurrent reads of dist/ during build can serve partial files. Write to a temp file then rename.

title: build: feeds.ts emitSitemap signature takes `content` but doesn't use it
body: Clean up signature.

title: build: no per-tag or per-author RSS feed
body: Ghost emits `/tag/foo/rss/`. Add.

title: build: no `_redirects` / Netlify config emit
body: From redirects.yaml when implemented.

title: infra: no GitHub Actions caching for bun install
body: Use actions/cache or oven-sh/setup-bun's built-in cache.

title: test: render engine smoke tests do not assert helper output byte-for-byte
body: Add specific helper tests with exact-string assertions.

title: test: example-build.test runs full build per run — slow if invoked frequently
body: Move to a separate `test:e2e` script.

title: dx: TypeScript path alias `~/*` works in tsc but not always at runtime in Bun without explicit config
body: Bun honours `tsconfig.compilerOptions.paths` natively. Verify by running on a clean machine and add to CI.

title: ghost-compat: `{{> partial}}` paths use POSIX separators internally; Windows users on raw paths fail
body: We normalise to `/` already, but spot-check tests on Windows.
